CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Continued To Apr-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMI FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon.

Defendant AMI FOODS, INC.'s Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the action is ordered dismissed with prejudice and judgment shall be entered consistent with the order of dismissal.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action With Prejudice And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Continued to January 24, 2025, on the court's own motion.

Judge: Victor M. Hwang. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/VMH)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2024 Continued To Feb-19-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AMI FOODS, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "DOMINO'S")'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's unopposed demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Continued To Dec-18-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Eric D. Despotes, Esq., CLAPP, MORONEY, VUCINICH, BEEMAN+SCHELEY (925) 734-0990 edespotes@clappmoroney.com, Attorney for Defendant AMI FOODS, INC. (erroneously sued herein as Domino's)

The Court address an error made in the tentative ruling.

Discussed, not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

DEFENDANT AMI FOODS, INC.'s DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Off calendar. The Despotes declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. Plaintiff is ordered to comply with the code. The response to the complaint is now due September 25, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
